notes: MarketingOS autonomous build queue — SocialMediaOS (SMOS) Phase 1, the Founder Story Session MVP (docs/products/marketingos/PRODUCT_HOME.md §5 Phase 1, §6 exact spec). TEXT-MODE ONLY: the audio-upload path (POST /sessions/:id/audio) is intentionally EXCLUDED because it depends on Cloudflare R2 env vars that are UNVERIFIED (§6 readiness gate). RE-QUEUED 2026-07-08 after the self-refinement spine (auto-registration + functional-proof gate + integration context) landed: the loop's original Phase 1 was false-done — the schema migration implemented the WRONG spec (generic ingestion schema), the route/UI modules imported non-existent service exports + a missing ./ai-council.js and were never mounted. The corrected §6 schema ships directly in this re-queue PR (delicate DROP of the empty mis-built tables — non-destructive, zero rows). The route + UI modules are re-queued as SELF-CONTAINED single-file modules (no external service imports) so they COMPOSE and the functional-proof gate can prove them LIVE. Both are pre-registered in config/auto-registered-product-modules.json so the boot auto-mounts them and module-health verifies them before the step can be done.
phase2_notes: PHASE 2 QUEUED 2026-07-09 (docs/products/marketingos/PRODUCT_HOME.md §5 Phase 2 — Social Content Calendar): four composing single-file steps — schema (marketing_content_atoms / marketing_brand_voice_profiles / marketing_calendar_slots, FK to Phase 1 tables), a LOCAL/no-AI brand-voice fingerprint+scorer service, the calendar/atoms JSON API, and the SSR calendar + atom-library UI. Route + UI modules are pre-registered in config/auto-registered-product-modules.json so the boot auto-mounts them and the functional-proof gate proves /api/v1/marketing/calendar + /marketing/calendar are LIVE before each step flips to done. Phase 3 (Video Clip Workflow) is intentionally NOT queued — its §5 spec has a BLOCKED dependency (FFmpeg on Railway + Whisper word-timestamps UNVERIFIED); it enrolls once those are verified.

steps.task: Correct §6 Phase 1 schema (five founder-story tables) — supersedes the mis-built 20260707 generic-ingestion migration.
steps.spec: Shipped directly in the re-queue PR (delicate DROP of the empty, never-shipped mis-built tables then CREATE per §6 exactly; idempotent CREATE ... IF NOT EXISTS). @ssot docs/products/marketingos/PRODUCT_HOME.md.

steps.task: New, SELF-CONTAINED Express route module exporting `registerMarketingSessionRoutes(app, deps)` (also a default export) that mounts the Phase 1 SocialMediaOS session API from docs/products/marketingos/PRODUCT_HOME.md §6 Route List, TEXT-MODE ONLY (do NOT implement POST /sessions/:id/audio — audio depends on unverified R2 storage). Do NOT import services/marketing-coach.js or services/marketing-content-engine.js or any ./ai-council.js — those are false-done stubs; implement the coaching/extraction/generation logic INLINE in this one file using deps.callCouncilMember for all AI and deps.pool for all DB. deps = { pool, requireKey, callCouncilMember, logger, baseUrl }. Derive owner_id from the authenticated request (req.user?.id, else req.body.owner_id/req.query.owner_id) and 400 if absent. Endpoints, ALL guarded by deps.requireKey: POST /api/v1/marketing/consent (insert a marketing_consent_records row from { consent_type, consent_text }, return { id }); POST /api/v1/marketing/sessions (require a valid consent_record_id — 400 if missing/unknown — insert a marketing_sessions row with input_mode='text', session_type='coaching', status='active', return the row); POST /api/v1/marketing/sessions/:id/coach (append the user message to coach_messages_json, call deps.callCouncilMember with the §6 Stage-1 coach system prompt + history, append the reply, detect a hook moment [energy/specificity/numbers], return { response, hookDetected, hookText }); POST /api/v1/marketing/sessions/:id/extract (set status='extracting', call deps.callCouncilMember with the §6 Stage-2 extraction prompt over transcript_text/coach messages, INSERT the returned items into marketing_content_extractions with their extraction_type/raw_text/confidence_score/source_quote, return them); POST /api/v1/marketing/sessions/:id/generate (set status='generating', for each extraction call deps.callCouncilMember with the §6 Stage-3 generation prompt using the caller's marketing_channel_profiles brand_voice_json, INSERT marketing_content_pieces rows [platform,format,content_text,status='draft',generated_by_model], set status='completed', return the pieces); GET /api/v1/marketing/sessions/:id (session state); GET /api/v1/marketing/sessions/:id/content (all marketing_content_pieces for the session with status); PATCH /api/v1/marketing/content/:id (body { action:'approve'|'reject'|'regenerate', hint? }: approve/reject set status; regenerate re-runs the Stage-3 prompt with the hint, bumps regeneration_count, returns the updated piece); GET /api/v1/marketing/sessions/:id/export (return the approved pieces as a formatted downloadable text pack with Content-Disposition attachment); GET /api/v1/marketing/channel-profile (get-or-create the caller's marketing_channel_profiles row by owner_id); PUT /api/v1/marketing/channel-profile (update channel_name/niche/brand_voice_json/audience_json/posting_cadence_json).
steps.spec: Single self-contained ESM server module. This code is AUTO-REGISTERED via config/auto-registered-product-modules.json (entry already present: path routes/marketing-session-routes.js, register registerMarketingSessionRoutes) — do NOT edit server.js or any startup/boot file. Use parameterised SQL via deps.pool ($1,$2… — NEVER string-interpolate into SQL). Use ONLY the exact §6 table + column names (marketing_consent_records, marketing_sessions with coach_messages_json JSONB, marketing_content_extractions, marketing_content_pieces, marketing_channel_profiles — all keyed on owner_id where applicable) and validate every enum against the §6 CHECK values (consent_type, session_type, input_mode, status, extraction_type, platform, format). Consent MUST exist before a session is created. This is a SERVER module: ZERO top-level references to window/document/localStorage/fetch-on-load or any browser global. Wrap every handler in try/catch and respond JSON { ok:false, error } with an appropriate status; never leak stack traces or secrets. callCouncilMember is async (role, prompt) => string — parse its text output defensively (it may wrap JSON in prose; extract the JSON array/object). Named export registerMarketingSessionRoutes + default export. Start with the standard `// SYNOPSIS:` + `@ssot docs/products/marketingos/PRODUCT_HOME.md` header.
steps.last_error: no_commit_sha (claimed pass without proof — treated as failure)

steps.task: New, SELF-CONTAINED server-rendered UI route module exporting `registerMarketingSessionUiRoutes(app, deps)` (also default) that serves the Phase 1 SocialMediaOS screens from docs/products/marketingos/PRODUCT_HOME.md §5 as HTML pages: GET /marketing (landing/dashboard — start a new session); GET /marketing/session/new (consent + session setup — posts to /api/v1/marketing/consent then /api/v1/marketing/sessions); GET /marketing/session/:id (coaching conversation, text input; posts to /api/v1/marketing/sessions/:id/coach and shows the coach replies + HOOK DETECTED flags); GET /marketing/session/:id/content (review + approve generated pieces; PATCHes /api/v1/marketing/content/:id); GET /marketing/session/:id/export (download the content pack via /api/v1/marketing/sessions/:id/export). Clean, conversion-minded, single-column layout consistent with the product's visual tone.
steps.spec: Single self-contained ESM server module, AUTO-REGISTERED (config entry already present: path routes/marketing-session-ui-routes.js, register registerMarketingSessionUiRoutes) — do NOT edit server.js or any boot file. CRITICAL SSR RULE: this file is imported by Node on the server, so it MUST have ZERO top-level references to window/document/localStorage/navigator/fetch or any browser global. Each route handler returns a COMPLETE HTML document as a template-literal STRING; ALL client-side behaviour (form submit, fetch calls to the /api/v1/marketing/* endpoints, approve buttons) lives ONLY inside a <script>…</script> string embedded in that returned HTML — never as top-level module code. No database access in this module (pages call the JSON API from the browser). No fabricated stats/testimonials/pricing. Escape any interpolated dynamic text. deps may be unused but keep the (app, deps) signature. Named export registerMarketingSessionUiRoutes + default. Start with the standard `// SYNOPSIS:` + `@ssot docs/products/marketingos/PRODUCT_HOME.md` header.

steps.task: MarketingOS Phase 2 (Social Content Calendar, docs/products/marketingos/PRODUCT_HOME.md §5 Phase 2) schema migration: create the three new tables that back the content-atom library, brand-voice profile, and 30-day calendar. Compose with the existing Phase 1 §6 tables (marketing_sessions, marketing_content_pieces, marketing_content_extractions) via owner_id and foreign keys — do NOT alter or drop any Phase 1 table.
steps.spec: Idempotent Postgres migration using CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS only. ALL id/created_at/updated_at columns are DB-DEFAULTED (id UUID DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY, timestamps DEFAULT NOW()) — do NOT expect the app to supply them. Tables: (1) marketing_content_atoms — owner_id TEXT NOT NULL, session_id UUID REFERENCES marketing_sessions(id), source_extraction_id UUID REFERENCES marketing_content_extractions(id), atom_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (atom_type IN ('hook','story','insight','cta')), text TEXT NOT NULL, tags JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]'::jsonb, reuse_consent_level TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'session_only' CHECK (reuse_consent_level IN ('session_only','90d','perpetual')), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(); index on (owner_id). (2) marketing_brand_voice_profiles — owner_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE, profile_json JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb, source_session_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(). (3) marketing_calendar_slots — owner_id TEXT NOT NULL, content_piece_id UUID REFERENCES marketing_content_pieces(id), scheduled_date DATE NOT NULL, platform TEXT, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'planned' CHECK (status IN ('planned','approved','used')), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(); index on (owner_id, scheduled_date). Start with a `-- SYNOPSIS:` comment and `-- @ssot docs/products/marketingos/PRODUCT_HOME.md`.

steps.task: MarketingOS Phase 2 brand-voice service (§5 Phase 2, 'brand voice profile built from ≥3 approved sessions, not manual' + 'brand voice fingerprinting: score new content against voice profile'). SELF-CONTAINED ESM module — no external service imports. Export async buildBrandVoiceProfile(pool, ownerId) and scoreContentAgainstVoice(profile, text). Fingerprinting is LOCAL/deterministic — NO new AI calls per §5 cost-control ('runs locally using stored session data').
steps.spec: Single self-contained ESM module. buildBrandVoiceProfile(pool, ownerId): read the owner's APPROVED marketing_content_pieces (join via marketing_sessions on owner_id; treat status/approved flag defensively), require >= 3 distinct approved sessions or return { ok:false, reason:'insufficient_sessions', sessionCount }. Compute a deterministic fingerprint with plain JS ONLY (no AI, no network): avg sentence length, avg word length, top N recurring non-stopword phrases/tokens, punctuation/emoji cadence, hook-pattern counts — store as profile_json; UPSERT into marketing_brand_voice_profiles (INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (owner_id) DO UPDATE) with source_session_count; return { ok:true, profile, sessionCount }. scoreContentAgainstVoice(profile, text): pure function returning { score: 0..1, notes: string[] } comparing the text's metrics to the stored profile (no DB, no AI). Use parameterised SQL ($1,$2 …) via the passed pool — never string-interpolate. Guard everything in try/catch and return structured errors, never throw raw. Named exports buildBrandVoiceProfile + scoreContentAgainstVoice. Import ONLY node: builtins / installed packages / existing repo-relative files. Start with `// SYNOPSIS:` + `// @ssot docs/products/marketingos/PRODUCT_HOME.md`.

steps.task: MarketingOS Phase 2 calendar/atoms JSON API (§5 Phase 2 New routes). SELF-CONTAINED Express route module exporting registerMarketingCalendarRoutes(app, deps) (also default export). deps = { pool, requireKey, callCouncilMember, logger, baseUrl }. Endpoints, ALL guarded by deps.requireKey and scoped to the caller's owner_id (req.user?.id else req.body.owner_id/req.query.owner_id; 400 if absent): GET /api/v1/marketing/calendar (return this owner's marketing_calendar_slots joined to marketing_content_pieces for a 30-day window, default from today); POST /api/v1/marketing/atoms (insert a marketing_content_atoms row from { atom_type, text, session_id?, source_extraction_id?, tags?, reuse_consent_level? } — validate atom_type + reuse_consent_level against the schema CHECK values — return the created row); GET /api/v1/marketing/atoms (list this owner's atoms, newest first, optional ?atom_type filter). May import services/marketing-brand-voice.js (buildBrandVoiceProfile/scoreContentAgainstVoice) to expose an optional POST /api/v1/marketing/brand-voice/rebuild that rebuilds the profile — but do NOT import any non-existent module.
steps.spec: Single self-contained ESM server module, AUTO-REGISTERED via config/auto-registered-product-modules.json (entry pre-added: path routes/marketing-calendar-routes.js, register registerMarketingCalendarRoutes) — do NOT edit server.js or any boot file. ZERO top-level browser globals (server module). Parameterised SQL only ($1,$2 …) via deps.pool — never interpolate. Use ONLY the exact Phase 2 table/column names (marketing_content_atoms, marketing_calendar_slots, marketing_brand_voice_profiles) + Phase 1 tables; ids/timestamps are DB-defaulted so INSERT WITHOUT them and RETURNING * to read back. Validate enums against the schema CHECK values and 400 on violation. Wrap each handler in try/catch → JSON { ok:false, error } with a sensible status; never leak stacks/secrets. Named export registerMarketingCalendarRoutes + default. Start with `// SYNOPSIS:` + `// @ssot docs/products/marketingos/PRODUCT_HOME.md`.
steps.last_error: no_commit_sha (claimed pass without proof — treated as failure)

steps.task: MarketingOS Phase 2 UI (§5 Phase 2 UI screens: content calendar view + atom library browser). SELF-CONTAINED server-rendered route module exporting registerMarketingCalendarUiRoutes(app, deps) (also default). Serves: GET /marketing/calendar (a 30-day content-calendar grid that fetches /api/v1/marketing/calendar in the browser and lets the user mark a scheduled_date per piece); GET /marketing/atoms (atom library browser that fetches /api/v1/marketing/atoms and can POST a new atom). Clean single-column layout consistent with the Phase 1 /marketing pages.
